affected_variables_or_families: Begin/end month variables for Medicare, Medicaid, and Other coverage; associated edited monthly indicators; associated recoded monthly indicators; corresponding status flags
practical_consequence: Naive month-level coverage, spell, and churn construction is not defensible for 2018-2023 without applying the official correction logic.
mainly_affects: monthly indicators; spell begin/end variables; status flags
evidence_basis: Official user note states the error is present in the 2018-2023 SIPP data files.

official_note_title: Health Insurance Begin/End Month Status Flag Error
affected_variables_or_families: Status flags for health insurance begin/end month variables
practical_consequence: Not-in-universe coding on begin/end status flags can be wrong; flag-based filters are unsafe until corrected.
evidence_basis: Official note says users should recode impacted status flags to 0 when corresponding begin/end variables are missing.

practical_consequence: Some Medicaid spells have invalid end months; raw spell termination dates are not fully reliable in 2018-2022.
evidence_basis: Official note gives explicit recode rule when EMD_BMONTH exceeds EMD_EMONTH.

official_note_title: Processing Error Effects on Uninsured Care
affected_variables_or_families: Uninsured care variables and corresponding status flags
practical_consequence: Some observations that should receive uninsured-care questions are incorrectly not in universe; uninsured-care outcomes are not prototype-ready without custom repair/imputation after monthly correction.
evidence_basis: Official note says users should first correct monthly health insurance variables and then impute uninsured-care values themselves.

practical_consequence: These are incomplete annual indicators and may underestimate coverage by type; they should not be used to estimate annual coverage.
evidence_basis: Official note explicitly says the screener variables should NOT be used to generate annual health insurance estimates.

affected_variables_or_families: EPR_SCRNR, EMC_SCRNR, EMD_SCRNR, EML_SCRNR, EOT_SCRNR not released on public-use starting with 2022 SIPP
practical_consequence: Cross-year code cannot depend on _SCRNR variables beyond 2021 public-use files; annual work must shift to recodes.
evidence_basis: Official note says the screener variables are not being released starting with 2022 SIPP public-use data.

affected_variables_or_families: RHICOVANN, RPRIVANN, RPUBANN, RMCAIDANN, RMEDCAREANN, etc.
practical_consequence: Annual recodes are available only from 2022 onward, so they cannot be used for a simple 2018-2024 stacked annual specification without back-construction.
evidence_basis: Official note says the 2022 SIPP added a set of annual health insurance recodes.

practical_consequence: RMARKTPLACE does not identify coverage type; it can flag private, Medicaid, or other exchange-obtained coverage.
evidence_basis: Official note states that RMARKTPLACE does not indicate the type of insurance a respondent had.

affected_variables_or_families: Other health insurance line and related "other coverage" indicators such as EOTMTH
practical_consequence: Other coverage is substantively ambiguous; it should not be treated as a clean policy category without extra classification work.
evidence_basis: Official note says there is ambiguity as to the type of coverage respondents are reporting on the ?other? line.

affected_variables_or_families: Coverage-unit identifiers such as EHIUNT1, EHIUNT2, EMLUNT, EMDUNT
practical_consequence: Coverage-unit analysis is not available in the checked public-use schemas, so within-policy-unit targeting is not currently supportable from public-use SIPP alone.
evidence_basis: Official note says these variables are not being released on the public-use file; local 2018-2024 schemas do not contain them.

affected_variables_or_families: End-month spell variables for health insurance types in waves 2+ cases
practical_consequence: Processing changed starting in 2021 to reduce false transitions from incomplete feedback data, which introduces a cross-year comparability caution around spell construction.
evidence_basis: Official note says beginning in 2021 SIPP data processing was modified to address false transitions in coverage.

official_note_title: Missing Values for Some Health Insurance Variables
practical_consequence: Some plan-detail variables have incorrect missingness; these are not suitable for a first churn prototype.
evidence_basis: Official note identifies specific variables with incorrect missing values.
